title: remove Serialize from receive-only enums with #[serde(other)]
description: ContactPresence, DeliveryState, SpanType, ChannelPermissionTargetType, and QuotaScope all derive Serialize despite being receive-only server types. Because they use #[serde(other)] for unknown variants without storing the original string, re-serializing the Unknown variant produces the literal string "unknown" — not the original wire value. For ContactPresence and DeliveryState this is misleading (clients never send these to the server). For SpanType the hazard is concrete: users CAN construct SpanType::Unknown and serialize a RichBody with it, producing "unknown" as the span type in an outbound message, which violates the spec. Fix: remove Serialize from purely receive-only types (ContactPresence, DeliveryState, ChannelPermissionTargetType, QuotaScope); for SpanType either store the string in Unknown(String) like BodyType/ChatKind do, or remove Unknown from the serializable surface.
